source_actor: VTT Technical Research Centre of Finland (VTT)
occurrence_sentence: Finland's Quantum First: VTT to Acquire Finland's First Quantum Computer

source_actor: VTT Technical Research Centre of Finland (VTT)
occurrence_sentence: VTT to Acquire Finland's First Quantum Computer

[VTT Technical Research Centre](https://www.vttresearch.com/en) of Finland has launched a years-long project to establish an ecosystem with companies and universities with a view to acquiring the first quantum computer in Finland, according to a company [statement](https://www.vttresearch.com/en/news-and-ideas/vtt-build-finlands-first-quantum-computer).
source_actor: VTT Technical Research Centre of Finland

occurrence_sentence: The goal of the first, one-year stage of the project is to produce a functioning five-qubit quantum computer while augmenting the country's ability to design and build quantum computers and creating a foundation for future applications.
source_actor: VTT Technical Research Centre of Finland
VTT Technical Research Centre of Finland
occurrence_sentence: [VTT Technical Research Centre](https://www.vttresearch.com/en) of Finland has launched a years-long project to establish an ecosystem with companies and universities with a view to acquiring the first quantum computer in Finland, according to a company [statement](https://www.vttresearch.com/en/news-and-ideas/vtt-build-finlands-first-quantum-computer).

target_actor: VTT Technical Research Centre of Finland
occurrence_sentence: **Antti Vasara** , CEO of [VTT](https://www.vttresearch.com/en), [reminded](https://www.vttresearch.com/en/news-and-ideas/vtt-build-finlands-first-quantum-computer) that advances in quantum technology and computing are expected to enable societies to overcome challenges that are impossible for contemporary supercomputers, including pandemics similar to COVID-19.

Antti Vasara , CEO of VTT, reminded that advances in quantum technology and computing are expected to enable societies to overcome challenges that are impossible for contemporary supercomputers, including pandemics similar to COVID-19.
